company.name: BridgeBio Pharma
company.subtitle: Attruby $362M Y1 · ATTR-CM first-choice therapy · BBP-418 LGMD NDA H1 2026 · Genetic disease platform
company.cashRunway: Into profitability trajectory ($588M cash + growing Attruby revenue)

market.notes: Attruby 2025: $362M (Y1 US). Q4: $146M ($584M annualized). 7,804 unique Rx by Feb 2026. First near-complete (≥90%) TTR stabilizer. ATTRibute-CM: 49% hazard reduction in CVM/CVH at Month 30. 81% survival rate approaches age-matched population. Competes with tafamidis (Pfizer Vyndaqel $5B+). Differentiated: faster onset (1 month), better TTR stabilization. Global launches underway.

us.patientsK.note: Pfizer/BridgeBio 10-K 2024: ~120K US ATTR-CM (wild-type + hereditary), growing ~20%/yr post-tafamidis label expansion and cardiac scintigraphy awareness.

eu.patientsK.note: ESC 2023 ATTR registry + Pfizer EU: ~90K diagnosed/diagnosable ATTR-CM across EU5+UK; under-diagnosed vs prevalence estimates of 300K+.

row.patientsK.note: Alnylam/Pfizer ROW: ~60K treatable ATTR-CM (Japan high V30M hATTR endemic pockets + AU/CA commercial markets).

us.wtpPct.note: ~70% -- Medicare Part B/D covers Vyndaqel, Attruby, Amvuttra post-ATTR-ACT/HELIOS-B; commercial payors require scintigraphy confirmation.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~55% -- NICE/HAS reimburse tafamidis for NYHA I-II only; G-BA negotiated price cuts limiting access to severe genotypes.

row.wtpPct.note: ~40% -- Japan NHI covers tafamidis (V30M endemic); emerging Asia ex-Japan limited access.

eu.priceK.note: EU net ~$140K post HTA negotiation; UK NICE list $130K, Germany AMNOG ~$150K.

row.priceK.note: $90K blended: Japan NHI tafamidis ~$120K, emerging markets tiered ~$40-60K.

penPct.note: ~45% peak -- diagnosis rate expansion + HELIOS-B Amvuttra label unlock drives sustained uptake through 2030.

us.reachPct.note: Attruby (acoramidis) 15% of ~150K US ATTR-CM -- launched Feb 2025; competing vs Vyndamax (Pfizer) $4B + Amvuttra CM; ATTRibute-CM 81% survival.

us.priceK.note: Attruby WAC $244K/yr; net ~$260K oral convenience; Vyndamax parity strategy; BridgeBio co-pay program.

us.wtpPct.note: 55% -- ACC/AHA HF guidelines + Medicare Part D + commercial; formulary ramping; step sometimes after Vyndamax.

eu.reachPct.note: 10% of ~80K EU ATTR-CM -- EMA Feb 2025; NICE/G-BA ramping; vs Vyndaqel + Amvuttra established.

eu.wtpPct.note: 40% -- NICE + G-BA recommended; ASMR pending; HTA ATTR-CM anchored to Vyndaqel.

eu.priceK.note: $160K EU net -- UK/Germany ~60% of US; parity to Vyndaqel EU pricing.

row.reachPct.note: 3% of 300K ROW ATTR-CM -- Japan ex-US partnership Bayer; China NMPA pending.

row.wtpPct.note: 12% -- Japan NHI pathway via Bayer, rest limited access.

row.priceK.note: $65K blended -- Japan NHI ~$140K projected, EM access.

market.notes: FORTIFY Phase 3: 2.6pt benefit on NSAD (p<α), broad benefit across subgroups. FDA recommended traditional full approval NDA. NDA submission H1 2026. Ultra-rare neuromuscular disease. Orphan pricing expected ($200-400K/yr).

us.patientsK.note: MDA/NORD: ~5K US LGMD total across subtypes; LGMD2E (SGCB) ~500; LGMD2I/2A/2B larger subsets.

eu.patientsK.note: Orphanet EU-5: ~7K LGMD; TREAT-NMD registry; Sarepta SRP-9003 (LGMD2E) gene tx in trials.

row.patientsK.note: Global LGMD ~140K; ex-US/EU ~4K diagnosed; genetic subtyping gap in emerging markets.

us.wtpPct.note: ~50% -- ultra-rare subtypes; gene therapy one-time potential high WTP for confirmed genotype.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~30% -- EMA orphan; HTA will scrutinize one-time gene tx durability data.

row.wtpPct.note: ~10% -- AAV infusion infra limited; Japan/select EU-adjacent primary ROW.

us.priceK.note: LGMD gene tx modeled ~$2.5M one-time (amortize $500K/yr over 5yr); Sarepta SRP-9003 pipeline.

eu.priceK.note: ~$1.6M one-time post-HTA (amortize $320K/yr); outcomes-based common for gene tx.

row.priceK.note: ~$700K one-time blended (amortize $140K/yr); Japan ~$1.2M, emerging markets minimal.

penPct.note: ~15% peak -- subtype-specific gene tx (one per SGCB/SGCA/etc.); each addresses <15% of LGMD pool.

us.reachPct.note: BBP-418 (ribitol) Phase 3 FORTIFY LGMD2I/R9; 50% of ~1-2K US LGMD2I -- ultra-orphan, near-ceiling pending 2026 approval.

us.priceK.note: Projected WAC ~$400K/yr -- ultra-rare neuromuscular pricing anchored to SMA/DMD therapies.

us.wtpPct.note: 55% -- pre-approval; ultra-rare Medicare/commercial specialty tier; first approved LGMD2I therapy potential.

eu.reachPct.note: 35% of ~3K EU LGMD2I/R9 -- EMA orphan pathway post-Ph3; NICE HST + G-BA ultra-rare.

eu.wtpPct.note: 42% -- orphan HTA managed entry; specialty neuromuscular centers.

row.reachPct.note: 8% of ~10K ROW LGMD2I -- Japan NHI pathway, limited rest.

market.notes: Low-dose infigratinib Phase 3 positive for achondroplasia (most common form of dwarfism). Competes with Voxzogo (BioMarin, $500M+). Differentiated on dosing convenience. NDA/MAA submissions planned.

us.patientsK.note: LPA + MAGIC Foundation: ~10K US achondroplasia (~1:25K births); pediatric growing pool ~3-4K eligible for CNP analogs.

eu.patientsK.note: Orphanet EU-5: ~12K achondroplasia; pediatric eligible (<18yr, open growth plates) ~4K for Voxzogo.

row.patientsK.note: Global ~250K achondroplasia; ex-US/EU ~8K diagnosed & accessible (Japan PMDA approved, BMS ex-US partner).

us.wtpPct.note: ~70% -- BioMarin Voxzogo strong pediatric uptake; payors cover for open growth plates; parental advocacy high (LPA).

eu.wtpPct.note: ~55% -- EMA approved 2021; NICE/G-BA reimbursed with age restrictions; annual HTA reviews for pricing.

row.wtpPct.note: ~25% -- Japan reimbursed; LatAm/APAC slower access; out-of-pocket barriers in non-OECD markets.

us.priceK.note: Voxzogo ~$320K/yr WAC (weight-based daily SC); duration ~5-8yr until growth plate closure.

eu.priceK.note: EU ~$210K/yr post-HTA (~30-35% discount vs US); duration similar 5-8yr pediatric dosing.

row.priceK.note: $150K/yr blended; Japan ~$260K, emerging markets tiered pricing $50-100K.

penPct.note: ~40% peak -- pediatric-only eligibility + 5-8yr duration limits ceiling; adult indication in trials could expand.

us.reachPct.note: Infigratinib (FGFR1-3 inhibitor) Ph3 PROPEL3 achondroplasia; 30% of ~10K US achon pediatric; vs BioMarin Voxzogo (vosoritide) SOC.

us.priceK.note: Projected WAC ~$320K/yr -- anchored to Voxzogo $320K oral convenience parity.

us.wtpPct.note: 50% -- pre-approval; oral vs daily SC Voxzogo convenience; Medicare pediatric specialty + commercial.

eu.reachPct.note: 20% of ~12K EU achon -- EMA post-Ph3; NICE/G-BA pediatric rare.

eu.wtpPct.note: 38% -- HTA achon rare pediatric; oral convenience vs Voxzogo injection.

eu.priceK.note: $200K EU net projected -- parity to Voxzogo EU.

row.reachPct.note: 5% of ~60K ROW achon -- Japan NHI + AU PBS pathway.

market.notes: CaSR inhibitor for ADH1. Phase 3 positive. NDA/MAA submissions planned. Ultra-rare disease — orphan pricing. Also exploring chronic hypoparathyroidism.

us.patientsK.note: Calcilytix/BridgeBio + NIH: ~10K US ADH1 (autosomal dominant hypocalcemia type 1, CaSR gain-of-function); encaleret Ph3 target.

eu.patientsK.note: Orphanet ADH1: ~12K EU; under-diagnosed vs hypoparathyroidism broader pool.

row.patientsK.note: Global ADH1 ~50K prevalence; treatable ROW ~15K diagnosed.

us.wtpPct.note: ~70% -- orphan; Medicare/commercial will cover encaleret (BridgeBio Ph3) post-readout; Natpara legacy for hypoPT.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~55% -- EMA orphan pathway; Yorvipath (palopegteriparatide) Ascendis approved 2024.

us.priceK.note: $250K/yr anchored to Ascendis Yorvipath (palopegteriparatide) WAC 2024 + BridgeBio encaleret Ph3 analog.

eu.priceK.note: EU net ~$150K post HTA orphan negotiation.

penPct.note: ~25% peak -- orphan captive ADH1 subset within broader hypoPT pool.

us.reachPct.note: Encaleret (CaSR antagonist) Ph3 CALIBRATE ADH1; 55% of ~12K US ADH1 symptomatic -- ultra-orphan near-ceiling post-approval.

us.wtpPct.note: 60% -- pre-approval; first approved ADH1 therapy potential; specialty endocrine Medicare + commercial.

eu.reachPct.note: 40% of EU ADH1 -- EMA orphan post-Ph3.

row.reachPct.note: 10% ROW ADH1 -- Japan NHI pathway, EM access limited.

assets.name: TTR amyloid depleter antibody + combo pipeline

market.notes: New depleter antibody program to reverse ATTR amyloid deposits (not just stabilize). Clinic 2027-2028. If it works: Attruby (stabilize) + depleter (reverse) = complete ATTR-CM franchise. Also oral GLP-1 combo for potential expansion.

us.patientsK.note: Pfizer/BridgeBio 10-K 2024: ~120K US ATTR-CM (wild-type + hereditary), growing ~20%/yr post-tafamidis label expansion and cardiac scintigraphy awareness.

eu.patientsK.note: ESC 2023 ATTR registry + Pfizer EU: ~90K diagnosed/diagnosable ATTR-CM across EU5+UK; under-diagnosed vs prevalence estimates of 300K+.

row.patientsK.note: Alnylam/Pfizer ROW: ~60K treatable ATTR-CM (Japan high V30M hATTR endemic pockets + AU/CA commercial markets).

us.wtpPct.note: ~70% -- Medicare Part B/D covers Vyndaqel, Attruby, Amvuttra post-ATTR-ACT/HELIOS-B; commercial payors require scintigraphy confirmation.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~55% -- NICE/HAS reimburse tafamidis for NYHA I-II only; G-BA negotiated price cuts limiting access to severe genotypes.

row.wtpPct.note: ~40% -- Japan NHI covers tafamidis (V30M endemic); emerging Asia ex-Japan limited access.

eu.priceK.note: EU net ~$140K post HTA negotiation; UK NICE list $130K, Germany AMNOG ~$150K.

row.priceK.note: $90K blended: Japan NHI tafamidis ~$120K, emerging markets tiered ~$40-60K.

penPct.note: ~45% peak -- diagnosis rate expansion + HELIOS-B Amvuttra label unlock drives sustained uptake through 2030.

us.reachPct.note: TTR amyloid depleter (anti-TTR mAb, preclinical-Ph1) 5% -- next-gen disease reversal beyond stabilization/silencing; early-stage risky.

us.priceK.note: Projected WAC ~$250K/yr -- anchored to ATTR class; premium TBD on clinical data.

us.wtpPct.note: 20% -- pre-clinical; if reversal shown, premium over Attruby/Amvuttra; payor tier speculative.

eu.reachPct.note: 3% EU ATTR-CM -- EMA post-Ph3 late 2020s; behind Attruby launch.

eu.wtpPct.note: 15% -- HTA pathway TBD; novel depleter MOA evidence bar high.

row.reachPct.note: 0.5% ROW -- Japan NHI pathway pending Ph3.

mega_bear.narrative: Attruby loses to tafamidis on price/access + pipeline delivers only niche orphans
events: ✗ Payers restrict Attruby in favor of generic tafamidis (2030s)
✗ BBP-418 LGMD, infigratinib, encaleret all approved but tiny markets
✗ TTR depleter fails in preclinical — Attruby alone insufficient
→ Attruby peak $2-3B only, pipeline adds $500M, overvalued at $15B

bear.narrative: Attruby grows but shares ATTR-CM with tafamidis + orphan revenue small
events: ✓ Attruby grows to $2B but tafamidis retains majority share
✓ LGMD + achondroplasia + ADH1 approved — combined $500M peak
~ Depleter program slow to advance
→ Total peak $3B — $15B market cap = 5x peak, fair but no upside

base.narrative: Attruby becomes ATTR-CM standard of care + 3 rare disease launches + depleter advances
events: ✓ Attruby $4B+ peak — displaces tafamidis as first-line in ATTR-CM
✓ BBP-418, infigratinib, encaleret all approved — rare disease franchise
✓ TTR depleter enters clinic — disease reversal potential
→ $5B+ peak revenue across franchise, approaching profitability

bull.narrative: ATTR franchise dominance (stabilize + deplete) + rare disease portfolio + $8B+ peak
events: ✓ Attruby $6B+ — dominant globally in ATTR-CM, displaces tafamidis
✓ Depleter antibody works — Attruby + depleter = complete disease modification
✓ 4+ approved rare disease products — genetic disease platform validated
✓ Revenue $8B+, highly profitable, M&A target

psychedelic_bull.narrative: ATTR-CM cured + rare disease franchise + Roche/Novartis acquires at $150+
events: ✓ Attruby + depleter = functional cure for ATTR-CM — $10B+ franchise
✓ 5+ approved rare disease medicines — BridgeBio model validated
✓ Platform generates next wave of genetic disease therapies
✓ Roche or Novartis acquires for $30-40B to own genetic disease platform

lastData.title: Attruby $362M Y1 + 7,804 Rx + 3 NDA-track programs
points: 7,804 unique patient Rx by Feb 2026, 1,856 prescribers
ATTRibute-CM OLE: survival + disease stabilization data at ACC 2026
BBP-418 LGMD: FORTIFY positive, NDA H1 2026 (traditional approval)
Infigratinib achondroplasia: Phase 3 positive, NDA planned
Encaleret ADH1: Phase 3 positive, NDA planned
Ervogastat (DGAT-2, licensed from Pfizer): Phase 2 combo planned

nextCatalyst.title: BBP-418 LGMD NDA submission + Attruby revenue trajectory

risks: $15B market cap with $362M revenue — priced for perfection
Tafamidis (Pfizer Vyndaqel) has massive installed base + first-mover advantage
ATTR-CM market share split uncertain — will Attruby truly displace tafamidis?
BBP-418/infigratinib/encaleret are ultra-rare — small revenue individually
Still loss-making despite $362M revenue — SG&A for launch is high
Debt on balance sheet from prior years
TTR depleter is preclinical — years from clinical proof
Alnylam patisiran + Ionis eplontersen competing in ATTR space

catalysts.title: Infigratinib achondroplasia NDA/MAA submissions
catalysts._rationale: NDA/MAA submissions planned for H2 2026 following successful Phase 3 PROPEL 3 data.

catalysts.title: BBP-418 LGMD NDA Priority Review decision
catalysts._rationale: NDA submitted March 30, 2026 with Priority Review potential and Fast Track designation
